meta_title: For Students: portfolio building to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Build Your Portfolio: Escape Beginner Tutorial Hell (Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop building basic apps. Learn how to craft a professional developer portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to land your first software engineering role.
content.answer_block: You are stuck because you are building tutorials, not products. Break the cycle by solving a real-world pain point, deploying it, and documenting the architecture. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to iterate on complex logic, shifting your focus from syntax to scalable, production-ready software systems.
content.intro:
The screen glows, but the code feels hollow. You've followed a dozen 'Build a To-Do App' tutorials, yet when you stare at an empty IDE, the blank page mocks your ambition. You aren't lacking talent; you are trapped in the 'tutorial purgatory' of low-stakes projects that never see the light of production.

It is time to pivot from student to builder. By leveraging enterprise-grade workflows like Antigravity and Claude Code, you will stop writing code that mimics others and start engineering solutions that solve genuine problems. This is where your journey from a learner to a hired developer truly begins.

steps.title: Define a Real-World Problem
steps.description: Stop building clones. Identify a manual process you do daily and design a tool to automate it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Architect. I need to build a tool that solves [INSERT PROBLEM]. Create a feature specification for a MVP that focuses on high-impact, low-complexity functionality.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project roadmap for a [PROJECT TYPE] with a focus on modular architecture.'

steps.title: Scaffold with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, ensuring your project structure follows industry best practices from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a production-ready repository for a full-stack application using [FRAMEWORK]. Include CI/CD config, linting, and a clean folder structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the project directory and configure the environment variables.'

steps.title: Implement Core Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Focus on the 'Unique Value Proposition' of your app. Let Claude Code handle the boilerplate while you architect the logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the core business logic for [FEATURE]. Use clean code patterns and ensure test coverage for edge cases.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Refactor the current logic to handle state management using [LIBRARY] patterns.'

steps.title: Add Observability and Logging
steps.description: Real developers don't just write code; they monitor it. Add telemetry to your app so you can debug in production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate structured logging and error tracking into my Express/Next.js backend. Explain how to monitor these logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Add middleware to capture and log all API request latency and error statuses.'

steps.title: Document and Deploy
steps.description: Your portfolio is your resume. Document the 'Why' behind your architecture in a professional README.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md for this project, highlighting technical challenges faced, architecture choices, and how to run the app.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Summarize the technical decisions made in this repository for a hiring manager to read.'

pro_tips: Never commit code without a corresponding test; treat your test suite as your primary documentation.
Focus on 'depth over breadth'—a single app that handles auth, payments, and database relationships is worth ten simple landing pages.
Use Claude Code to perform 'code reviews' on your own work by asking it to find security vulnerabilities or performance bottlenecks.
Always maintain a 'Decision Log' in your repo to explain why you chose specific libraries or architectures.
Deploy early and often—a live URL is 100x more impressive to recruiters than a perfect GitHub repository.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, add one complex feature (like real-time data or OAuth) and share your project on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the technical hurdles you overcame.
content.conversion_block: You are closer than you think to becoming a developer who ships. Stop watching and start building the future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
